channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Wes Anderson’s The Phoenician Scheme is arriving sooner than you think, and I'm hugely excited for Michael Cera’s cameo
item.description: We finally have a release date for Wes Anderson's next movie – here's what we know about The Phoenician Scheme so far.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Is this the iPhone SE 4? New Apple device seemingly appears in leaked images as revised release date emerges
item.description: The iPhone SE 4 could launch slightly later than expected, but leaked photos have given us a clear look at the possible design.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The irritating but amusing irony of Google’s Gemini interface
item.description: Look, if you’ve read this column for long now, you know I’m extremely guarded with my enthusiasm for Gemini and the other similar large-language-model AI answer-bots.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: PlayStation State of Play live build-up – start time, rumors, and all our predictions and hopes ahead of tonight's show
item.description: Sony's next State of Play presentation happens today, February 12, and we're tracking all the rumors and predictions in the build-up to the show
item.dc:creator: rob.dwiar@futurenet.com (Rob Dwiar), TechRadar

item.title: Huawei Cloud fuels Saudi Arabia’s AI ambitions at LEAP 2025
item.description: At LEAP 2025, Huawei Cloud demonstrated its commitment to Saudi Arabia’s AI leadership, showcasing its Riyadh Region as a pivotal hub for AI development in the Middle East, Central Asia, and Africa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New US CIO appointments, February 2025
item.description: Movers & Shakers is where you can keep up with new CIO appointments and gain valuable insight into the job market and CIO hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NBN Co delivers free 600,000 fibre upgrades, with plenty more still to come – here's how to get yourself in the next batch
item.description: NBN Co has announced nearly 600,000 homes have taken advantage of its free fibre upgrade program with many more homes are already eligible. Find out if you're one of them and the best plans to sign-up for.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta’s laid-off ‘low performers’ defend themselves on LinkedIn and Reddit
item.description: Meta underwent another big round of layoffs on Monday, cutting 3,600 jobs, or roughly 5%, of its total workforce. Between 2022 and 2023, the tech giant eliminated 21,000 positions, nearly a quarter of its workforce, and continued to reduce staff in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Monday.com aims to be an ‘AI-First’ platform with latest enhancements
item.description: Monday.com has accelerated its push into artificial intelligence (AI) with the announcement this week of its AI vision, which includes three areas of focus: AI Blocks, embedded Product Power-ups, and a forthcoming Digital Workforce of AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 ways Tesla stands to win from Elon Musk’s war on the U.S. government
item.description: Elon Musk has long railed against the U.S. government, saying a crushing number of federal investigations and safety programs have stymied Tesla, his electric car company, and its efforts to create fleets of robotaxis and other self-driving automobiles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel
item.description: The intelligence community has increasingly prioritized OSINT in recent years. Now, House lawmakers are creating a new subcommittee dedicated to the discipline. The post OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Biggest Google Pixel 9a leak so far reveals colors and seems to confirm the death of the camera bar
item.description: Not keen on the rumored redesign for the camera of the Pixel 9a? Bad news: another leak appears to confirm the bar is being ditched.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This tiny 2TB portable SSD has a unique feature that makes it the perfect storage companion for your iPhone
item.description: ShiftCam’s Planck SSD delivers lightning-fast storage, perfect for creators capturing 4K ProRes video on their iPhones.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cisco data center switches feature baked-in security for AI, networking duties
item.description: Cisco has unwrapped a new family of data center switches it says will help customers more securely support large workloads and facilitate AI development across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: EU seeks to invest €200 billion in AI
item.description: The European Commission announced the mobilization of €200 billion (about $207 billion) for the InvestAI plan at the AI Action Summit in Paris on Tuesday, with the aim of enabling “open and collaborative development” of artificial intelligence in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Streaming is finally profitable. It offers a lesson in patience
item.description: Just a couple of years ago, pundits were warning of streaming’s demise. From Netflix to Spotify, these companies were burning through cash. How could they keep operating? Now, almost all of the streamers have made it to positive profits.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Supermarket Simulator Pro is no more as a number of 'spam' games are removed from the PS Store
item.description: It looks like Sony may have quietly removed a number of "spam" shovelware games from its PlayStation Store.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: The International Olympic Committee says the first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JD Vance rails against 'excessive' AI regulation in a rebuke to Europe at the Paris AI summit
item.description: U_S_ Vice President JD Vance has told a Paris summit on artificial intelligence that the Trump administration will “ensure that AI systems developed in America are free from ideological bias,” and that the U_S_ would “never restrict our citizens’ right to free speech.”.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk’s $97 billion OpenAI bid could give the DOGE chief even more power in the AI race
item.description: A group of investors led by Elon Musk has given OpenAI an unsolicited offer of $97.4 billion to buy the nonprofit part of OpenAI. An attorney for the group submitted the bid to OpenAI Monday, the Wall Street Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk furious as judge shuts down DOGE access to Treasury payment system
item.description: The US Treasury Department’s payment servers hold the tax returns, social security data and bank account numbers of every adult citizen of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Internet upgrade part of a move towards 400/800G connectivity
item.description: The industry transition to 400 Gigabit Ethernet networking took a big step forward this week when the world’s leading Internet exchange operator announced plans to upgrade its New York backbone to 400G.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your ‘new’ Seagate data center hard drive is likely a used one
item.description: A growing number of buyers have reported purchasing supposedly new Seagate data center-grade hard drives, only to discover that they had been previously used for thousands of hours.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk calls for annual firing of judges after blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called for the annual firing of judges following an early Saturday decision from a judge stating that the Treasury Department should bar access to its payment systems to anyone besides “civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Visual Studio Code unveils Copilot code editing suggestions
item.description: A just-released update of Visual Studio Code, version 1.97, introduces a preview of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, an AI-powered feature that predicts the next edit the user wants to make.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World
